Event: Christopher Evan Welch dies before Season 1 shooting is complete
Explanation: His death harms his own career/life and forces costly rewrites with no benefit to him or the production.

Event: Showrunners rewrite last several Season 1 episodes to refer to his character without appearing
Explanation: Rewriting mitigates disruption and keeps story coherent, modestly benefiting show and others despite constraints.

Event: Writers incorporate his death into the show as a major Season 2 plot point
Explanation: Using his death as a plot point adds narrative value and audience engagement with some creative gain, no evident harm.

Event: Peter pursues extremely dubious business positions (e.g., $30M ostrich farm, invisibility companies, GM cranberry fungus)
Explanation: His unusual investments appear financially reckless, risking his own capital and stakeholders’ money without shown payoff.
By auditing his positions she gains understanding and oversight, with neutral-to-positive effects on others.

Event: Laurie informs others about the dubious investments (ostrich farm, invisibility firms, fungus cotton)
Explanation: Revealing these positions helps correct or manage them, protecting the firm and stakeholders.

Event: Peter acts as an eccentric mentor to the Pied Piper team before his death
Explanation: His mentorship aims to help the team succeed and presumably enhances his own reputation/position.

Event: Peter, as a reasonable authority, tolerates the Pied Piper crew and wants them to do well
Explanation: His supportive oversight benefits the team while maintaining his role and potential upside.

Event: Pied Piper team comes to Peter begging for emergency funding
Explanation: They reveal vulnerability and dependence, slightly weakening their position while seeking help that could also allow Peter to profit.

Event: Peter spends most of the meeting analyzing every item on the Burger King menu
Explanation: He uses their urgent meeting for his own analytical process, delaying their funding and causing them stress while gaining insight.

Event: Peter reveals he has devised a brilliant sesame-seed futures plan to fund the start-up team
Explanation: His plan yields large profit for himself and directly provides needed funding to the team.

Event: Peter uses Gavin Belson’s past friendship (implied network) as part of his business life
Explanation: The prior friendship likely affords social and business advantages to both without shown harm.
